fact: December 1 is exactly 31 days away from New Year's Day - that's like counting down one number every single day of the month!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying their last acorns and nuts, creating secret treasure maps in their tiny brains that they'll somehow remember all winter long.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities by December 1, making it feel like bedtime when you're barely done with homework!
December 1 marks the beginning of meteorological winter, which means scientists officially consider it winter even if there's no snow yet.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, December 1 kicks off summer - so kids there are getting ready for beach days while northern kids are bundling up!
Many deciduous trees have completely naked branches by this date in northern climates, looking like giant skeletons reaching up to grab the gray winter sky.
The winter solstice is only 20 days away from December 1, meaning each day is still getting shorter and darker in the north.
Reindeer and caribou in the Arctic have grown their thick winter coats by now, making them look like fluffy walking carpets with antlers.
December 1 falls during Sagittarius season, represented by an archer - perfect timing since this zodiac sign loves adventure and the holidays are full of exciting plans!
In many cold regions, the first hard frost has already killed most insects, leaving spiders with empty webs that collect morning frost like tiny crystal dreamcatchers.
December 1 is exactly 11 months after New Year's Day, making it the perfect time to panic about all those resolutions you forgot about!
